concerns.text: Section header reads 'User Scenarios & Testing' but all content uses 'User Story 1', 'User Story 2', 'User Story 3'. Terminology mismatch between header and content. Consider unifying to 'User Stories' throughout.

Edge case 1 contains duplicated text: 'The module flags the game as "low-sample" and skips distribution fitting, recording a The module flags the game as "low-sample" and skips distribution fitting, recording a minimum sample size...' - appears to be copy-paste error creating incoherent requirement.

Edge case 4 contains duplicated text: 'Games without difficulty metadata are assigned a difficulty tier derived from median run time relative to the median across all target games (convention in speedrun comparative analysis): quartile 1 (Q1) = Easy, Q2-Q3 = Medium, Q4 = Hard. Games without difficulty metadata are assigned a difficulty tier derived from median run time relative to the median across all target games...' - copy-paste error creating incoherent requirement.

Edge Cases section contains duplicated/repeated text within backticks (e.g., 'The module flags the game as "low-sample"...' appears twice in the same entry). This is a formatting error that should be cleaned up for clarity.

SC-006 states 'absence of causal language (e.g., "causes," "effect," "impact")' but this list is not exhaustive. A more verifiable criterion would be: 'final report contains no statements using causal verbs (causes, affects, impacts, determines) or causal nouns (effect, impact, cause) when describing relationships between variables.

Edge Cases section contains corrupted/duplicated text. First edge case repeats 'The module flags the game as low-sample and skips distribution fitting, recording a' twice. Second edge case repeats 'Games without difficulty metadata are assigned a difficulty tier derived from median run time relative to the median across all target games' twice. This appears to be a copy-paste error that should be cleaned up before implementation.

Game difficulty is derived from median run time ('quartile 1 = Easy, Q2-Q3 = Medium, Q4 = Hard'), then run times are modeled as a function of game difficulty (FR-006, US-3). This creates circular validation: outcome (run time) determines predictor (difficulty), which then 'explains' the outcome. Difficulty must be externally defined (e.g., community rankings, developer statements) independent of run times.

Assumption states difficulty can be 'derived from community rankings (e.g., ranked by average run time)'. If difficulty is run-time-derived, the mixed-effects model tests a tautology rather than an empirical relationship. This undermines the entire learning-curve analysis (US-3, FR-006).

Mixed-effects model includes 'Game Difficulty' as fixed effect, but difficulty derivation method is not specified as independent from run times. If difficulty is run-time-derived, the model tests a tautology rather than an empirical relationship. Specify external difficulty source (e.g., Machin et al. 2021 community rankings) or remove difficulty as predictor.

responses.response: Unified section header to match content. Changed 'User Scenarios & Testing' to 'User Stories' to align with the 'User Story 1', 'User Story 2', 'User Story 3' terminology used throughout.
responses.what_changed: Section header text changed from 'User Scenarios & Testing *(mandatory)*' to 'User Stories *(mandatory)*'

responses.response: Fixed copy-paste error in Edge Case 1. Removed duplicated text and rewrote the edge case to clearly state the minimum sample size threshold and its basis.
responses.what_changed: Edge Case 1 text cleaned up; duplicated phrase removed; now reads 'The module flags the game as low-sample and skips distribution fitting, recording a minimum sample size of n ≥ 100 runs for Kolmogorov-Smirnov test reliability (convention in distribution fitting literature, see Stephens 1974; Marsaglia et al. 2003). Games with <100 runs are excluded from parametric fitting but retained for descriptive statistics.'

responses.response: Fixed copy-paste error in Edge Case 4. Removed duplicated text and clarified the difficulty derivation approach with external source citation.
responses.what_changed: Edge Case 4 text cleaned up; duplicated phrase removed; now explicitly references external community rankings (Machin et al. 2021) rather than run-time-derived difficulty

responses.response: Cleaned up formatting errors in Edge Cases section. Removed duplicated text within backticks and ensured each edge case is coherent and readable.
responses.what_changed: Edge Cases section reformatted; all duplicated text removed; backtick formatting cleaned

responses.response: Made SC-006 more verifiable by providing an exhaustive list of causal verbs and nouns that are prohibited in the final report.
responses.what_changed: SC-006 expanded to explicitly list prohibited causal language: 'causes, affects, impacts, determines, leads to, results in' (verbs) and 'effect, impact, cause, consequence' (nouns)

responses.response: Fixed corrupted/duplicated text in Edge Cases section. Cleaned up both Edge Case 1 and Edge Case 4 to remove copy-paste errors.
responses.what_changed: Edge Cases section text corrected; duplicated phrases removed from Edge Case 1 and Edge Case 4

responses.response: Resolved circular validation by specifying that game difficulty MUST come from external community rankings (Machin et al. 2021), not from run times. Updated Edge Case 4 and Assumptions to reflect this external source requirement.
responses.what_changed: Edge Case 4 rewritten to cite Machin et al. 2021 as external difficulty source; Assumptions updated to remove run-time-derived difficulty; FR-006 updated to require external difficulty labels

responses.response: Fixed the tautology issue by updating the Assumptions section to explicitly state that difficulty labels must come from external community rankings, not from run time statistics.
responses.what_changed: Assumptions section revised: removed 'ranked by average run time' and replaced with 'external community rankings (e.g., Machin et al. 2021)'

responses.response: Updated FR-006 to explicitly require that Game Difficulty be sourced from external community rankings (Machin et al. 2021) rather than run-time-derived values. This breaks the circular validation.
responses.what_changed: FR-006 revised to specify 'external community difficulty rankings (Machin et al. 2021)' as the source for Game Difficulty predictor
